question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline the benefits of being an accredited Living Wage employer.

To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any investment allocated by his Department to individual community-based projects in the South Antrim constituency between 1 April 2025 and 31 March 2026, including the streams through which funding was delivered.
To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on the policy relating to the funding and deployment of speed indicator devices through policing and community safety partnership structures.
answer_text: The Department of Justice and Northern Ireland Policing Board jointly fund Policing and Community Safety Partnerships (PCSPs), via a Joint Committee, to identify and help address local community safety issues in each council area. The Department for Infrastructure (DfI) is policy lead for road safety and, while it does not fund SIDs, it has provided a memorandum on the DfI website which details the use of these - Speed Indicator Devices (SIDs) DEM 191/23 | Department for Infrastructure. PCSPs support a wide range of community safety initiatives. The funding decisions taken reflect the need to support that broad remit while ensuring that road safety remains an integrated part of their overall programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will meet with two of my constituents and me to discuss a recent diagnosis of Ewing sarcoma in their family, further to the request I sent to him on 16 February 2026.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on preparations for the Communities in Transition programme as part of the Executive programme to tackle paramilitarism, criminality and organised crime.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether any assessment has been made on the cumulative impact that US Israeli-led military attacks in the Middle East is having on the cost of living locally.
To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the timeline for bringing forward an Irish Language Strategy.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will deliver another financial support package for brown eel fishers in Lough Neagh, in the early part of the 2026-27 financial year.
answer_text: The current support is a special, one-off scheme to provide financial easement to enable stakeholders to consider the current challenges and adapt operations and fisheries management measures for next season. Such challenges would include the identification of new, alternative markets for brown eels. I do not therefore envisage at this time that another financial support package for brown eel fishers in Lough Neagh will be delivered during the 2026-27 financial year.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the work their Department is undertaking through the Communities in Transition programme.
answer_text: The Communities in Transition (CIT) programme has delivered £29 million of support across eight designated areas since 2019, making a real difference in local communities. This year CIT continues to operate across the same areas, supporting 35 projects with a total budget of £2.9 million. Building on learning to date, CIT projects focus on addressing hard-hitting issues including Mental Health and Addictions, Raising the Aspirations of Young People, Exploitation, and the Impact of Paramilitarism on the Physical Landscape. Restorative Practice and Ex-Prisoner support are delivered on a regional basis.It is important the projects tackle the issues that matter within our local communities.In 2024/25, 107 of the project activities addressed poor mental health, 92 addressed the lack of aspiration in young people and 87 addressed the impact of poverty and deprivation 85 addressed peer pressure, 52 improved relationships with the PSNI and 16 addressed the impact of paramilitarism on the physical landscape.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will consider introducing legislation to prohibit local authorities from entering into waste incineration contracts that require payment of financial penalties if a minimum amount of waste is not delivered for incineration, or for connected purposes.
answer_text: Waste incineration is a viable treatment for waste that cannot be recycled and would otherwise be landfilled. At this stage, legislation relating to waste incineration is not currently under consideration.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail which agency within his Department formulated the eligibility criteria relating to the £100K of financial assistance for the Lough Neagh brown eel fishermen.
answer_text: The scheme of financial assistance for Lough Neagh Brown Eel Fishermen and hauliers was developed by officials within my Department.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will consider the introduction of a regional protocol to redress any health inequalities caused by lack of access to Shared Care Agreements for children and adults who have had to seek a private diagnosis for ADHD.
answer_text: There is currently no specifically commissioned service for ADHD in Northern Ireland. While there is some provision available across the Health and Social Care system which has evolved in response to population demand and expertise within Health & Social Care teams, it is inconsistent and there are lengthy waiting lists across Trusts. In response, my Department has commissioned a needs assessment on ADHD. The findings of which will help inform options for the development of a commissioned ADHD service across Trusts.  The needs assessment will be published in November 2025 and will include recommendations about shared care arrangements.The implementation of recommendations and commissioning of service provision will be dependant on the availability of funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the All-Island Strategic Rail Review.
answer_text: My officials continue to work closely with colleagues in the Department of Transport, Translink, Irish Rail, and the National Transport Authority to consider the Review’s recommendations. This work has included the preparation of a Project Prioritisation Strategy, which will be published later this year. My officials are also working closely with Translink who have undertaken several feasibility studies linked to recommendations in the Rail Review. I am currently considering these studies and intend to publish a summary report of them later in the Autumn.Delivering the Review’s recommendations will not be without its challenges, given the scale of the interventions and the transformative change required. However, my Department remains committed to exploring all available funding avenues, recognising the significance of this work in driving economic growth, enhancing connectivity, and addressing long-standing regional imbalances.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will create a financial support package to compensate Lough Neagh commercial fishermen for loss of income and to address financial hardships arising from the full termination of the 2025 eel fishing season.
answer_text: My Department is actively progressing a request for financial support following the suspension of the brown eel fishery. A business case is currently under way to support a scheme. The silver eel fishing still continues to operate.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when Winter Fuel Payments will be issued to recipients.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail when the funding will be approved to commence construction on the Birch Hill Centre for Mental Health at the Antrim Area Hospital site.
answer_text: Following the announcement of the outcome of the 2025 UK wide Spending Review in June, the Department of Finance has commissioned a Budget exercise that will determine the capital budgets for Northern Ireland Department’s for the years 2026-27 to 2029-30. Whilst I remain hopeful, my ability to commit to funding capital projects, including the important Birch Hill Centre for Mental Health, will depend on the Department of Health capital allocation from the Budget process. The draft Budget outcome is not expected to be confirmed until later this year and will inform the development of the Department of Health capital investment plan which will set out the projects to be taken forward over the next ten years.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the Investment Strategy for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: We are currently considering a final draft of the Investment Strategy. We hope to be able to bring it to the Executive for approval in the near future. The timeline for approval and publication of the Investment Strategy will then be a matter for the Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on her Department's support for employee ownership.
To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to provide an assessment of the current ecological status of Lough Neagh.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the Transport Strategy 2035.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for their assessment of the motion passed in the Assembly on 6 May 2025 which expressed support for extending the right to vote in elections for president of Ireland to all Irish citizens on the island of Ireland.
To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on an anti-poverty strategy.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the implementation of the Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the Historical Institutional Abuse redress scheme.
To ask the Minister of Finance to provide an update on the Public Sector Transformation Board.
answer_text: My Executive colleagues and I have been clear that we want to both transform the delivery of public services and put public finances on a sustainable footing.I established, and appointed members to, an interim Public Sector Transformation Board. The interim Board provided its advice to me on Stage 2 proposals it received from departments. Last week I tabled my recommendations to Executive colleagues in relation to the first tranche of funding from the £235m Transformation Programme. Ministers had a positive discussion around the proposals recommended for funding and the process undertaken by the interim Board to develop its advice. I am also scheduled to meet the Secretary of State, later today, to finalise the Terms of Reference for the interim Board. This will allow the Executive to continue its vital work on the transformation of our public services.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on a Racial Equality Strategy post-2025.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline the next steps for the Programme for Government 2024-2027, following closure of the consultation.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to provide an update on the appointment of the Commissioners under the Identity and Language Act 2022.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the work progressed under the auspices of the North South Ministerial Council.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on work to recruit commissioners to the Office of Identity and Cultural Expression.
To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the role of Social Enterprise NI in developing the social economy.
